2606.14270.title: Robust Fall Recovery for Armless Bipedal-Wheeled Robots Via Force-Guided Learning
2606.14270.paper_summary: Fall recovery is critical for autonomous legged locomotion. Existing methods have demonstrated that some legged robots, such as humanoids and quadrupeds, are capable of fall recovery from diverse postures by utilizing arms or coordinating multi-legs to generate support forces. Without arms or other legs to provide supportive assistance, a bipedal-wheeled robot must rely solely on the actuation of its legs, making recovery particularly difficult. To address this, we introduce FTSR (Force-guided Teacher-student framework with Stage-wise Rewards). The force-guided method constructs an external auxiliary force during simulation training that correlates directly with the robot's real-time height, explicitly formulating this force as an optimizable constraint. Through constrained reinforcement learning, the policy is guided toward reducing force dependency gradually and increasing the body height, developing internal recovery strategies despite having no arms for support. Height-progressive stage-Wise rewards progressively structure posture stabilization during recovery and transition to sustained locomotion, integrated with teacher-student architecture distilling privileged knowledge of force effects and recovery dynamics. After simulation training, the policy is deployed on a physical armless bipedal-wheeled robot and extensively evaluated. Experiments confirm robust and reliable fall recovery under diverse challenging conditions, demonstrating strong environmental adaptability and motion robustness, while maintaining full post-recovery motion capability. The framework also generalizes effectively to a high-DOF humanoid, confirming its practical generalizability. The project page is available at https://2350575870.github.io/force-guided.github.io/
2606.14270.paper_summary_zh: 秋季恢復對於自主腿部運動至關重要。現有的方法已經證明，一些腿部機器人，如人形機器人和四足機器人，能夠通過利用手臂或協調多條腿來產生支撐力，從各種姿勢中恢復摔倒。沒有手臂或其他腿部提供支持的雙足輪式機器人必須僅依賴其腿部的驅動，使得恢復變得特別困難。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了FTSR（基於力量引導的師生框架，具有階段性獎勵）。這種力量引導的方法在模擬訓練期間構建了一個外部輔助力量，該力量與機器人的實時高度直接相關，明確地將這種力量公式化為可優化的約束。通過受約束的強化學習，策略被引導逐步減少對力量的依賴並增加身體高度，儘管沒有手臂支持，仍然發展出內部恢復策略。高度漸進的階段性獎勵逐步結構化恢復過程中的姿勢穩定性，並過渡到持續運動，結合師生架構提煉力量效應和恢復動態的特權知識。在模擬訓練後，該策略被部署在一個無臂的雙足輪式機器人上並進行了廣泛評估。實驗確認在各種挑戰性條件下具有強大和可靠的摔倒恢復能力，顯示出強大的環境適應性和運動穩健性，同時保持完整的恢復後運動能力。該框架也有效地推廣到高自由度的人形機器人，確認其實際的普遍性。項目頁面可在 https://2350575870.github.io/force-guided.github.io/ 獲得。
2606.14270.author: Haidong Hou et.al.
2606.14270.authors: Haidong Hou, Zhangguo Yu, Tao Han, Hengbo Qi, Khaleel Ghazal, Yu Zhang, Yidong Du, Xuechao Chen, Fei Meng
